text: At this point I’m going to get a degree in medicine and do it myself 😐
shouldn't they know this. why do we have to tell them
I had mine 6 months ago and now my pain in worse. anyone else?
This is the lady who told me I had endometriosis after surgery... Incredible woman

Same 🫠 going through all that made me feel so out of control… pain, heaviness, low energy all the time. I tried so many things and still felt stuck. A friend in a forum mentioned Eat Like a Woman Protocol written by loren green. I followed it and everything shifted—30 lbs gone, no more bloat, clearer mind, steady energy. kinda wild this isn’t talked about more, love it)
I had my surgery with a general gynaecologist, was in surgery for 30 minutes, told there was no endo and pain is in my head. No photos either to take to a specialist.
I had pouch of Douglas endo and it was so painful
Recently had one but didn’t tell me where they looked , was not aware of this helpful information hope this reaches someone before they have it for a better outcome

question why is nobody doing MRIs? i had one done in Russia electively just to check if my lap surgery in Ireland missed any (they didn't thankfully), confirmed adenomyosis, checked the rest, all for an hour of my time and €100 privately. why is it not an option in Ireland?
Saw liz many years ago, very knowledgeable and skilled person.
I’m still so convinced my doctors missed endo, I don’t know that I can go through the mental process of requesting another one and if it doesn’t show it I’m lost again

Wow, we are so moved by the response to this video! Thank you to everyone sharing their own stories and helping us reach more people who need to watch this.
We’ll be posting more from our interviews with Liz soon, so stay tuned. For those asking, Liz is an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist based in Wales who provides private virtual consultations. If you’re looking to connect with her, let us know and we can help.
I had a gynaecologist and she found endo and adenomyosis last week. They removed the endo and have now asked if id like to try Dienogest before deciding if I want a hysterectomy. I’m 40.
what if you ask for a 360 an they say no are they allowed to say no?
sounds good but ... very unlikely they would take any notice of what we ask for, as the patient
During my lap they didn’t do either just had a look at it and left it😅

It would be really helpful if you could provide a letter on guidance of what we could ask for. Paper trails are so important when you’re constantly getting told no. I would love to do this myself, but don’t have the medical qualifications or knowledge to! ♥️
Thank you for sharing your expertise and experience xx
cyst draining and ablation is what my gyne recommended. luckily I had already done a lot of research and now am waiting first ever surgery with an endo specialist at a BSGE accredited centre.
Thankyou for explain him booked in for 13 July surgery so helping with clarity - I’m in Aus :)
Thank you Liz🥰

had an ablation in 2013, worked well until 2021, now i have stage 4 endo, frozen pelvis, adhesions and bowel adhesions and ashermans syndrome with the top of my uterus thickening. would not recomend ablatiom at all
I was told I was getting an excision but during my lap they did ablation. This definitely puts things into perspective.
Exactly what the surgeon told me. In my sigma the excision was needed to remove the disease…
I had one ovary endo excised and one ablated to try and preserve as much fertility as possible.
How much is it and do you have it in Sheffield/manchester
More information if possible, would this cover NIreland as well?
Girls need to understand it’s not embarrassing to talk about your periods too. Awareness on what is not normal is needed. I put up with this for too long before finding out I had endometriosis.
Great idea to bring education into schools, it is much needed! xx

women's health has a long way to go before it's really truly in hand. I'm stage 4 fibrotic endometriosis. as a kid I had to go home on the first day of my period. as I got older I would have an episode every few months where I'd be vomiting then.pass out. I think maybe I had endo for the whole time

I was lambasted by a GP at a surgery when I asked if it was possible that endeometriosis was still active at age 69. Bowel issues and kept giving me laxatives. I moved house and different area and surgery and a year later age 70 have now been referred for bowel ct scan with contrast after a failed coloncoscopy. I had a previous bowel resection and my bladder was stuck to my bowel. Full hysterectomy and all this surgery was done when I was mid 30s.

I had a pelvic MRI and on my results letter it literally just said 'This is a normal pelvis'. That was it. Turns out my bowel and left ovary were adhered to my pelvic wall! Yeah....that's completely normal 😳🙄
My endo specialist told me and mri can either over diagnose or under diagnose endometriosis, is that true?
Does the radiologist you work with look at scans privately, if so do you know what they charge?
I had a private MRI that’s how I got a proper diagnosis years ago stage 4!

So I had an mri done the radiologist report aside from noting an endometrioma notes everything else as normal however I did go see specialist couple days ago and they will be calling me this week to schedule excision surgery after the Dr looks over the mri so expecting since he’s a specialist his read will be very different than the radiologist who likely lacks on spotting endo

Yep 13 and fainting I remeber very well. Being told to get a grip and take an admit powder !! All the time it was endeometriosis and properly diagnosed at age 22 but by that time it was in the bowel. Never think it’s normal always get a doctors opinion. But on saying that the first GP I saw put me on the pill to resolve the situation and again it only masked what was going on.
Askit Powder

You are an amazing woman-thank you so much for sharing🥰 So true, fatigue is so overlooked, especially when nothing shows up in bloods. GP has always wanted to say 'how is your life style.' Never hear the facts because trained to hear- Alcohol, not looking after yourself! xx
I’ve had two huge surgeries. Had the hysterectomy for Adeno in the end. I can sleep for days and still tired.

Living with endo for 30 years… has lighted, then told I had it but then lied and said we got it all it won’t come back. 10 yrs of not being believed, went private , whole insides stuck and bowel resection and hysterectomy. Year on, still in pain - the bleeding ! One thing stopped 🥺
I spend 70% of my time in bed I get tired just sweeping my room
The best endo nurse you are Liz ❤️sharee bush ❤️
Thank you🥰
Fantastic information. Thank you
I think I would just show him/her this video , you say it all, swiftly and succinctly 🙏🏻🔥💚💫
Hello Lizzie ❤️❤️❤️

Hi, I have just asked my consultant if all of this was checked in my first surgery when I had an ablation. I had a few pictures back in 2024. I have just had them come back and say the following- Endometriosis outside pelvis is quite rare. Bowels were not involved at the time of laparoscopy, though we will have an update with MRI. Its not possible to check diaphragm completely with laparoscopy due to position of upper abdominal organs.
I have gone back again and asked what was checked and mentioned about wanting a 360 assessment. also they will look to do an excision this time. apparently it wasnt possible the first time because my womb was too angry and inflamed and bleeding. Wondering if what my specialist has said is right or should I be seeking to speak to another specialist? many thanks.
Thank you very much for this 😊
Great info🥰

I asked them to remove my hernia (was told my colorectal specialist that it’s routine to remove after laparoscopy) and to remove my Lutheral cyst as I was getting severe pain and they said they couldn’t find my hernia and that the cyst shouldn’t be causing me paid so they didn’t remove it?????

May I please ask if fibromyalgia can mimic endo pain? I started off with endo that lead on to fibromyalgia. I am having debilitating endo pain again sadly it got put down to fibromyalgia as I had a laparoscopy in 2012 that was “clear” this was a gynaecologist appointment last year. I’m waiting on a second opinion.
I dont have a pouch of Douglas, it's been obliterated 😂😂😂

is it normal to have all my pain i. the right side , lower back , groins get so painful ! pain after going to toilet .. they won’t scan me 😢i’m on waiting list for laparoscopi, my right side drives me crazy xx

I went through the exact same. I knew they were wrong. They ordered an MRI after to prove me wrong (it wasn’t even the right MRI). They discharged me with IBS. So I paid £15k for a private surgery a year later and it took 6 hours to remove most of it and ended up staying inpatient for a week with an ovarian suspension. It was so bad there is no way they couldn’t have spotted it. I’m still so angry and I’m absolutely taking this further now x
Why do surgeons operate when they know they don’t have enough training? It seems crazy this is still going on.
First mistake was going to general gynaecologist 😭. Most of them are trained enough for endometriosis
my exact experience 🙃
I had varies procedures and they couldn’t see any endo, when I had my hysterectomy the endometriosis was all over my bladder bowel gall bladder pelvic wall, they kept telling me that I didn’t have it

hi, I have umbilical endometriosis. im suffering with back and leg ache too. my doctor has just diagnosed me with polymyalgia rheumatica with the aches. people over 60 get that. he wants to start me on strong steroids but im in two minds to start as I think its linked to endo. can u help me x

i have had severe pain ever since i had my first period and alot more other symptoms when i was 9 i started seeing doctors when i was 12/13 i am now 16 been seen many times by many people iv always been told i dont have endometriosis since i have never had severe bleeding i have a mri to see if i have endometriosis hopefully soon will it always show up on mri if i do?

I have ademyosis and endo - years ago I was told scarring on POD and had 3 prem babies. constant issues with bowels and just had cameras for both ends and bowel was agony and results clear - it was said by gastro it could be behind and pushing or fused and hence why one side of my stomach /right side is large and other is small - I literally feel stuck- on wait list for laproscopy and they offered me a hysterectomy at 41 ! havent been in to see anything - i have declined and asked for new coil whilst under and to remove endo where can and 360 view @docmap

I went through laparoscopic surgery for total hysterectomy and salpingectomy because abnormal perimenopausal bleeding. Went in they said they took the tubes but hysterectomy wasn’t able to be completed due to stage 4 endo with frozen pelvis and obliterated cul de sac. Was sent home with pictures and progesterone and told if not experiencing pain to wait for menopause.😫
@Sammy Joe
so what about new pain 12 weeks post total hysterectomy?
this happened with my second surgery :( i’m scared to ever have surgery again
my pain has worsened since being on the coil and it’s to the point it’s affected my ability to walk at times 😭 my surgery isn’t until next year cause i need bowel and gynae surgeons for my endo :(
@Eden| Endo Awareness/support🎗
My endo specialist told me my pain wasn’t my endo , he wouldn’t even acknowledge that my uterus was tethered to my bowel. Feeling very dismissed
It showed up on my mri, I had one surgery but only to remove my ovaries

Had an Mri ( not for a endo specialist ) they said they can not see anything my gyno suspects i have endo ( I also have been diagnosed with PCOS ) I am crippled in pain most days out of the month . It started about 4 years ago it would come for afew days and leave now I feel like its always there . I got the nerve blocking injection last Friday and the pain is worse ended up in a&e again got a ct scan they Said they cant see anything. Im due to get a laparoscop but the waiting list is 12 months . I am not sure if its a 360 scope . If endo is not found do you have any advice moving forward ? thanks
I didn’t know this I had an ablation 15 years ago

I got assigned an endometriosis nurse at the end of last year. She's fantastic however she is based over 200 miles away and covers a massive area. I agree with Liz, everyone with endo deserves a local endo nurse!
Hi Liz - Am I able to message this page with some questions for you please? I had ablation on bilateral utero sacral endometriosis over 10 years ago 😬
You are your own biggest advocate 🫶🏻

Completely agree AI can be so helpful!
I had a laparscopy in December and they found adhesions on my bowel and my ovary was adhered to my pelvic wall. No active endo but did find old pelvic scarring. They managed to release some adhesions on my bowel but couldn't do anything with my adheared ovary as it was risking my utera (I never know how to spell that 😂)
I went back for my follow up in March and told my consultant I was still in lots of pain. She completely dismissed my pain and told me that my adheared ovary wouldn't cause me that much pain (I'm struggling to walk as I have sciatic type pain in my leg and my ovary literally feels like it's pulsating and being ripped out of my body!) She just told me to loose weight and the pain should pass!
This didn't sit right with me so I used Chat GBT to deep dive into my issues. Through this, I was able to educate myself on a lot of things. It told me that I should be referred to an endo specialist as my laparscopy was incomplete, there was a risk to my utera (that word again!), even though she had released some adhesions on my bowel.. that should have been done by a specialist and that due to my ovary being adhered to my pelvic wall, it's classed as pelvic distortion. All of these fit the criteria to be referred to an endo specialist, which she should have done right after my surgery 🙄
I then asked Chat GBT to create a letter I can send to my GP requesting for a second opinion and to be seen by an endo specialist.
The letter was brilliant and really clear. I sent it off not really expecting much back but within a week my GP called me and couldn't believe I had been dismissed like that and agreed with me that I should be referred to an endo specialist! She sent the referral off to the Endo specialist centre on Oxford as that's the closest place to. me and my referral has been accepted!! All this because of AI!
I now have the long wait to be seen but I would rather wait it out to be seen by a specialist than a general gynocologist that thinks loosing weight is the only key to becoming pain free! (I'm 14 stone so not too overweight either!) So thank you AI!
Oh hey 👋🏻
How can a appointment be booked please?
How much does it cost
I truly believe there now needs to be more endometriosis centres across the country to meet the demand

I had endo surgery just over a year ago, I was told they found lesions and removed them, I was still in pain that seemed to get worse and worse, had an ultrasound a couple of weeks ago and they found an 8cm Dermoid cyst on my ovary and several calcified cysts all over my ovaries that were blatantly missed or just ignored. I feel so broken by a system that just does not seem to care
My mri they said it was completely normal and it was extremely disheartening
6 months post op. still in loads of pain...whyyy!
I think we need to know who to go to in our area via NHS

the surgeons in newham literally told me they won’t operate on me because the scans show nothing and they’d hate to waste their time on something that isn’t there. i was diagnosed literally in april, the endo is behind my liver. if you feel that somethings wrong, keep pushing!!!
true
See this seems to be my problem, surgery by people who aren’t experts 😭
Got diagnosed with adeno after ultrasound and now waiting for my MRI to exclude endo… I don’t know what to expect and what to hope for anymore 🥹🥹
How do find one...

They said my mri show cervix ehrn I was removed I had two surgeries one endometriosis removed it and one after nerve damage, tech kept saying it was cervix my wonderful gp as like I’m calling prior surgeron got on phone with tech said reading this wrong look again turns out it was scar tissue

Iv been ignored over and over while they miss things. I had a team of gynaecologist around my bed 4 of them all reading the same mri. But yet all 4 came up with different findings and none of them were the same like all the answers were different 1 said it was in my uterus and don’t have endo were 2 others said no it’s she has it inside and out and it’s spreading another said no it’s just serface endo . Iv been told this last op they would be removing a lot of it they left it all in there I’m battling with them and getting no were I have all medical records with there findings over the years but no treatment. These illnesses do not stop they grown and grown and grown but yet they say mine hasn’t they don’t know what there doin

But yet won’t refer me to the rite specialist. They say they will do I don’t trust them . Never have they followed up anything after there findings. And say scans and mri are normal . I don’t believe they have a clue what there doin . I’m struggling to even now how to sue them or get a second opinion. Everything scan and test they do is normally but I’m riddled makes no sense

Been diagnosed since 2003 with endo adeno and pso for over 26 years iv had 7 surgeries but yet never treated just diagnosed. I believe it’s rite up in my chest as I have symptoms of this they refuse to send me specialist with all there finding and send me on my way . I have just had surgery on 2nd of may believing I was getting but they removed nothing but say it a server complex case but they will now remove my womb and uterus . They say it’s solves everything . Endo is in my body has been since 12 years old I’m now 44 . I get told it not real it’s in my head it can’t be that painful. I have bleed now sold 2 years due to fybroids . I have stomach issues bowl issues chest problems pain in back on chest in legs . I feel I’m in labour all day every day . No pain killer work I been describe so many different ones and all it’s done is give me oesophageal polyps as well as stomach ulcers since 1016 due to all meds killing my stomach off . I can feel stomach and bowl stuck together but yet mri says it’s not there blood are normals all are normal scans are normal but the findings after surgery are so bad it everywhere. Iv only just been told iv never been treated . I was admitted after surgery as they made things worse as they just irritated all my diseases. I suffer chronic pain daily . I told them I want to be referred or I will sue the hospital. They won’t refer me but yet now after 15 years of fighting have they agreed for hysterectomy with gyny now

they found my endo on several ultrasounds as well as on the MRI it was HUGE i had surgery in the beginning of march they did a laparotomy and even had to remove my appendix cuz it spread to there they game ne some pills that i need to take everyday and they told me i probably wont be getting my periods anymore but i have been bleeding lik on and off all the time for the past 2 months (id bleed for 4 days then stop for 2 then again for 3 and so on and on) and i gen dk what to do and sadly i am still in pain like most of the time idk what to do
I love thisss
This is amazing, I just done my dissertation on endo ! X
Hey 🫶🏻

My surgeon is bringing in a general surgeon as she believes that even though my MRI didn’t show endo in my rectum and bowels and bladder, they believe it still might be there. I’m so glad they’re being proactive.
just had my endometrioma removed after 8 years of being told it's in my head ,🎉

MRI show stage 3 endo laparoscopy show 0 still in horrendous pain living on nerve blockers and morphine nothing touching the pain still have flairs bladder bowel pelvic pain , I’m adamant I’ve got endo I’ll not stop until I have answers

I had transvaginal scan that showed I have a cyst, fibroids and the wall thickening she even asked if I have severe periods because the scarring was that bad and she was shocked when I said I've not had one in over 15 yrs yet the gynecologist still won't see me

Had an internal and external scan and it was normal, had my laparoscopy on Friday and they found stage 3 endo on my bladder, bowel, ovaries and wall lining, 16 years of fighting, I’m finally validated
This… I was 10 when I started mine!
You get used to running on 30% energy. I can't even remember what it feels like to not feel tired or in pain. It's a sad thing to realise and have to accept.
Nausea is a massively overlooked symptom too

The lethargy is horrific and nobody understands how hard it is to deal with. People see it as me "napping" all the time, it's involuntary, once that wall hits, it's time out, I have no choice, and because endo is invisible, everyone thinks there's nothing wrong with me and no reason for it.
Its my worse symptom
Chronic fatigue syndrome should automatically be added to your medical record once diagnosed with endo
Im running on sheer willpower at this point
my legs always feel like jelly and like they have weights on them
i described it as walking through treacle

Fatigue was one of the worst symptoms for me, you feel like you are letting people down and people think you’re lazy. The pain is an obvious symptom but fatigue was put down to so many other issues it was really difficult to know that it was endo causing it. Anyway, after laparoscopy and the coil and a different diet, I’m feeling much better and getting to exercise and do the things I love. Don’t give up ladies xxx

It’s one of my worst symptoms and so debilitating. I developed endo later in life. And used to be someone who could survive of 3 hours of sleep. Now I have this condition along with insomnia and lethargy. It’s so debilitating. I was so active and now the thought of leaving the house seems too exhausting 😫
lethargy is a symptom of many many issues in your body
Fatigue is what I struggle with the most. I count myself lucky not (yet) to have the excruciating pain that others describe, but still the constant fatigue is hard to live with.
The fatigue is one of mine no matter how much sleep I get I feel like I haven’t slept on the odd occasion I wake up feeling great but then it soon hits again
I could sleep for a month and wake up exhausted. I cannot describe the tiredness in words
The fatigue is something else 💀 even when unwell too it gets worse! I’ve got stage 4 it’s awful!

I have my lanyard and rollator due to endometriosis causing so much pelvic, back and leg pain massive difference with my local rail service now so friendly and kind..before I was suffering in silence even through my whole family told me I need support I finally listened and it's helped day to day now
I’ve asked for my photos, theres a 6 week backlog in getting them! Hoping to get a second opinion with these
i had a 12 minute surgery and a tap on the shoulder during recovery saying all good. 2 years later my organs are stuck together and im loosing my bowel.

I received 10 images from my laparoscopy and their timestamps span 3 minutes. Does that mean my procedure only took that long? Surely not, right…? 😟 (they didn’t find anything abnormal but I was diagnosed with adenomyosis 6 months later, fit every symptom of endo and have a family history of it so still strongly believe they have missed it)
With the pictures do you ask before or after your lap procedure?
Same in a 45 minute case if that also included a coil insertion? Is this still considered quick? X
I had to get mine via a SAR! Wouldn't be shared otherwise. Now waiting an ungodly amount of time to be seen by an endo centre to discuss removal/treatment!

I had a discharge letter only it stated nothing found but they did take a biopsy but I never received histopathology results never even saw surgeon after procedure and discharged currently fighting to get biopsy results
Iv had 3 surgeries n never had anyone photos each surgery was 3.5 hrs

get a second opinion off who tho im sick of battling with gp, ive had ultra scans bloods coil out, now waiting ct scan?? and got gynecologist in 2 weeks, I cant do anymore or tell them anymore, they look at me daft like im mellonadratic, they want to try live with it, but 10yr later still going round and round, I leave it and suffer 10 months then try get my head round it to explain. properly, but that many symptoms they just say yr bloods ye this ye that, basically pass the book and we battle with gp and hospital then, what do I need for them to find it please??
I asked to have access to my mri images and they said the file is too big?🤔🙃 I have to do a SAR just to see my own results 😳
My last surgery there were no pictures taken!
I asked for this. They didn’t follow through. I was so dissapointed.
So even on nhs I can request they take photos? What about biopsies?
was told there were no pictures taken and still waiting for my missing histopathology results which were never sent to gp or myself
I wish I saw this sooner 💔
Surgeons love giving things that could be later used against them in court.
Do you ask for pictures before or after your surgery?
I have my pics from surgery

Was very adamant on getting my pictures, one of the first things I asked for when I woke up from my laparoscopy. The surgeon told me that the printer on the floor wasn’t working so I couldn’t get my photos, I continued to ask and then he told me that none of the printers were working?! I had a follow up call about a month later after I emailed him and asked for a specialist and to get my photos as they must be on a file somewhere and was happy to print them off myself. Only then did he say that no photos were taken at all!
I had robotic surgery and asked for pics before the op he granted it then after the op he said there is no pics as it was with a robot and the robot dusnt take pics 😳
yeah you can get them, after you submit a SAR!
Not only do I have pictures, I have a full video.
over 6 years old , deleted 👀🙈
mine did'nt save properly... i have 1 photo, i had multiple spots of endo and lesions...
I’ve already had my preop 😭 am I too late?
What symptoms would present if I have endometriosis in the eye? I’ve had vision problems in my right eye for years and the opthomologisist cannot find anything wrong with my vision or eye.
Thank you. I listed all of these for my private appointment at the Manor in Oxford, and the specialist said yes to all 🙏
Thank-you as very helpful indeed
I either have adhesions or Endo on my diaphragm or both, it’s pretty much only thing it can be at this point.

Wish I seen this before my surgery on Tuesday 😩 I get 2nd degree inflammation in my eyes which I’ve been told is due to the endometriosis in my body and my surgeon told me after my laraproscopy she ‘just found it on my left side’ I was barely awake at this point haven’t seen her since, so nervous a lot has been missed 😞
Could you do a video on dermoid cysts? I have one large one and several small ones and I’m in so much pain and I feel like I’m not being taken seriously enough at all
I’d love that job girls definitely need more education about their body’s , health and wellbeing 🥰🥰🥰
Mine started at 10😓 was so so horrific in primary school
it missed it on me!!!!
